rationale.R1.2: The artifact draws a causal conclusion ('selection-based aggregation drives the gain') from a single ablation across three targets and asserts consistency with no regressions; logical coherence of these inferences is the most probable load-bearing concern under any scientific read of an open-ended 'what do you think?'
rationale.R1.3: The artifact explicitly motivates its evaluation by early enrichment (top 0.5–1% of ranked compounds) but then adopts ROC-AUC as the primary metric, which integrates over all decision thresholds and is insensitive to early enrichment specifically; this is a candidate metric–claim mismatch that would be central to an evidence-alignment review.
